title: Add clean command (implemented by interactive session)

Add usage examples to each CLI command's help output — e.g., session shows 'generalstaff session --budget=480  # overnight 8-hour run', cycle shows 'generalstaff cycle --project=myapp --dry-run'. Update the main --help too.
In dispatcher.ts, add a tiebreaker to scoreProjects: when two projects have the same score, prefer the one with fewer total_cycles (spread work more evenly). Add a test for this behavior.
In session.ts, if a cycle returns verification_failed, log a one-line summary to stderr with the reason so the user sees it even when output is scrolling. Add test.

Add a test that validates the full CLI help output includes all registered commands: session, cycle, status, stop, start, log, projects, init, history, doctor, clean. Ensures new commands don't get forgotten in help text.
Add a session.test.ts with tests for the writeDigest function: verify it creates the digest file, includes all cycle results, formats SHA ranges, and handles empty results array

In dispatcher.ts, add a function estimateSessionPlan(projects, fleet, budgetMinutes) that returns a preview of which projects would be picked and how many cycles each would get, without executing anything. Add test.
Add a formatDuration helper function to a new src/format.ts module that converts seconds to human-readable strings (45s, 2m30s, 1h15m). Use it in history and digest output. Add tests.
In work_detection.ts, add a function countRemainingWork(project) that returns the number of pending tasks (not just boolean hasMoreWork). Use it in the session summary output. Add test.
Add a projects.test.ts test that validates warnProjectPaths with a real git repo (use the GeneralStaff repo itself as the valid path) and a non-git directory

Wire estimateSessionPlan into the session command — before running cycles, print a preview table showing which projects will be picked and how many cycles each gets. Only show when not --dry-run. Add to session output.
Add a generalstaff task command to cli.ts with subcommands: 'task list --project=<id>' shows pending tasks, 'task add --project=<id> <title>' appends a new task to tasks.json. Include tests.
In the history command output, add ANSI color codes: green for verified, red for verification_failed, yellow for verified_weak, gray for cycle_skipped. Only colorize when stdout is a TTY.
Add a session.test.ts test that validates the empty-cycle guard exits after 3 consecutive empty diffs, and that capped projects get added to the skip set

In audit.ts, add a function summarizeCosts that counts reviewer_invoked events from PROGRESS.jsonl and estimates total token usage based on prompt_length. Wire into the history --format=compact output as an optional column.

Add a generalstaff summary command to cli.ts that prints a one-screen overview: total cycles across all projects, verified/failed/skipped counts, total duration, test count (run bun test --reporter=json to get count), and tasks remaining. Like a dashboard snapshot.

In projects.ts, add a validateHandsOff function that checks each hands_off pattern against the project's actual file tree (git ls-files) and warns about patterns that don't match anything — helps users catch typos in their hands-off config. Add test.
Add a test for extractChangedFiles in cycle.ts — verify it handles renames (diff --git a/old b/new), binary files, and files with spaces in their names
In state.ts, add a getProjectSummary function that aggregates fleet state + project state + remaining tasks into a single object. Use it in the status --json output for richer data. Add test.
Add a generalstaff version command (separate from --version flag) that shows version, bun version, platform, and projects.yaml location. Useful for bug reports.
